digest.greeting: Tuesday, July 14, Jean. Today has 10 timed calendar blocks, with the day anchored by the 11:00 AM Legal Change Management overlap, the 12:00 PM AmazonConnect/Venn Timeline and OCM deep dive, the 2:00 PM EPMO TL weekly catch-up, and late-day PM/SharePoint touchpoints. There are 6 carried-over items and no active reminders; the highest-leverage move is to turn the open operating threads into owners, dates, and one shared status path before the afternoon stack fragments them. Teams is rate-limited this morning, so Teams-specific context is degraded and the brief leans on Outlook and dashboard history.

items.label: Turn the PM team index, Planner, and weekly deck into one operating structure
items.detail: Keep shaping the Loop index, Planner updates, and weekly executive deck into one operating rhythm before today's EPMO TL and PM touchpoints create more parallel status surfaces.

items.label: Align BD portfolio review with Janet's transition touchpoint
items.detail: Keep the BD review, transition path, active work, risks, new intake, owner decisions, and transition next steps connected as one operating lane.

items.label: Prepare the next step on Universal Intake
items.detail: Matthew's thread added Jean as the BD project manager and called for a meeting before UI changes are made. If no one else schedules it, Jean may need to prompt the next step.

items.label: Review the new intake alerts before portfolio work
items.detail: Keep the Phase 2 and BD intake items routed to ownership or portfolio follow-up before they fragment across separate tracking paths.

items.label: Check the Mental Health Expansion charter deliverable
items.detail: Confirm whether the draft charter is ready for stakeholder review or needs an owner/date reset.

items.label: Start LA AI change-management testing
items.detail: Give the LA AI testing item a clean first pass now that several heavier July 13 follow-ups are closed.

sections.title: Today's Schedule
sections.note: Times are shown in Jean's Outlook working-day context. The 11:00 AM block is the main schedule risk; the 1:00 PM hour has a second overlap between CSO GLs Huddle and the external Latent Variables check-in.

items.label: OOO-Shelley
items.detail: Shelley remains out through Friday, July 17; route approvals or dependencies around that absence.

items.label: Sara in Dublin Office
items.detail: Sara is marked in the Dublin office Tuesday through Thursday, which may shape timing for any Sara-dependent decisions.

items.label: 1:1 Jhara & Jean
items.detail: Portfolio review, roadblocks, and next steps; useful setup for the later Legal Change Management and SharePoint conversations.

items.label: Legal Change Management conflicts with Blocked Space
items.detail: Jhara scheduled an IT/EPMO sync at the same time as Jean's protected block. Decide whether to attend live, delegate, or preserve the block and capture follow-up async.

items.label: AmazonConnect + Venn Tunneling - Timeline & OCM
items.detail: Sara and Danny set this as a high-priority project deep dive to review status, stakeholders, risks, blockers, and mitigation plans.

items.label: CSO GLs Huddle
items.detail: Calendar hold with Hassan as organizer; it overlaps the external Latent Variables check-in starting at 1:30 PM.

items.label: Latent Variables / Jean Garcia - Check In
items.detail: External Zoom check-in with Yashraj Patel; accepted and overlapping the back half of the CSO GLs Huddle.

items.label: EPMO TL Weekly Catch Up
items.detail: Weekly touchpoint with Janet, Eugenia, and Jorge. Use it to turn open carry-over threads into owners, dates, and a single status path.

items.label: Focus time
items.detail: Protected hour after the EPMO TL catch-up for cleanup or follow-through.

items.label: 1:1 Nicolas & Jean
items.detail: Regular 1:1 with Nicolas; good place to check PM operating structure, portfolio tracking, and open owner/date decisions.

items.label: SharePoint touchpoint
items.detail: Review how Jhara saves project files in SharePoint or OneDrive; tie the answer back to PM status and file governance.

items.label: Resolve the Legal Change Management 11:00 AM conflict
items.detail: The IT/EPMO sync overlaps Jean's protected block and follows yesterday's Legal Change Management movement. Decide whether to attend live, send a delegate, or ask Jhara for the decision points and next steps afterward.

items.label: Prepare the AmazonConnect/Venn OCM risk view
items.detail: Sara's invite frames the noon meeting as a high-priority project deep dive. Go in ready to clarify timeline, stakeholder alignment, blockers, OCM risks, and what needs mitigation after the meeting.

items.label: Use the EPMO TL catch-up to convert open threads into owners and dates
items.detail: The open list still spans PM operating structure, BD transition, Universal Intake, Phase 2 intake, Mental Health charter, and LA AI testing. Use the 2:00 PM catch-up to prevent those from becoming separate status tracks.

items.label: Shelley is OOO this week
items.detail: The calendar shows Shelley out from Monday, July 13 through Friday, July 17. Route approvals or dependencies around her absence.

items.label: Legal Change Management - IT & EPMO Sync
items.detail: Another Legal Change Management sync appears tomorrow; today's 11:00 AM decision should clarify whether this remains needed.

items.label: Marketing Projects Portfolio Review and Claude overlap
items.detail: The Marketing portfolio review and Claude session both sit at 11:00 AM, so tomorrow already has a prioritization decision.

items.label: PM Open Discussion: How to avoid going sideways on projects?
items.detail: Use today's PM operating-structure work to shape a cleaner discussion tomorrow.
